calendar.matters:
Petition For An Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Or, In The Alternative, Petition For Instructions Under Probate Code Section 17200 Directing Payment Of The California Department Of Health Care Services Creditors Claim; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bruce Feder and Will Doyle for and with Trustee, Siu Kin Choy. Mrs. Choy's daughter (Susan) and son (Warren) also present in the courtroom.
Charles J. Antonen for California Department of Health Care Services.

The Court noted that service/notice is not perfected, that it does not see relief under 850, and inquired if parties have considered mediation. Mr. Antonen stated that he is ready to go forward today, that this case is not amenable to mediation, that it is not about the dollar amount of the Department's (DHCS) claim, and there are other issues due to the positions of the parties. Mr. Feder stated that they did briefly discuss mediation, that the DHCS would not offer more, and the reason for proceeding is that there is law permitting exceptions to this case. Counsel agree that there is no dispute of facts, that material facts of what happened are all in front of the court, and that all parties agree to proceed without an evidentiary hearing. Counsel cited case law (Hertin, Arnold and Gonzalez) and presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to DHCS's inferences of Mrs. Choy's motivation to pay this claim, deviating from the Trust to keep money for herself, 3605 b, preemption, letter from CMS, congress' rule on payback, etc.

The petition for order under probate code section 850, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Court reporter: Carol Haraburda CSR 8052 (tel: 925-360-6065 email: chreporter@comcast.net).

Petition For An Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Or, In The Alternative, Petition For Instructions Under Probate Code Section 17200 Directing Payment Of The California Department Of Health Care Services Creditors Claim; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Continued To Mar-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions On Demand For Reimbursement To Department Of Health Care Services
Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions On Demand For Reimbursement To Department Of Health Care Services
Off Calendar Aug-26-2019 Continued To Oct-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder and Will Doyle for and with Siu Kin Choy (Petitioner).
Charles Antone from the Department of Justice on behalf of the Department of Health Care Services.
Susan Tu (Daughter of Ms. Choy) also present.

The Court stated that it has serious concerns about Probate Code 17200 being the appropriate vehicle, that the Trustee has some discretion and raises the issue of why the Court should act prior to the Trustee exercising her discretion. Mr. Feder stated that the Trustee is exercising her discretion and that she is asking the Court for instructions to deny the claim. The Court stated that it is the Trustee's responsibility and that if she does not want to act, she can resign and she can deny it. Mr. Feder stated that the Trustee has already refused to pay it, that the Court has the ability to look at this and decide if this is the right way, that he believes it has been done in other Courts, that 17200 allows a Trustee to ask these types of questions and receive guidance from the Court, spoke of the pay back provision and stated that Counsel disagree as to what this means. The Court stated that it will not step in until it knows that the Trustee has exercised her discretion and it is ripe, that Counsel can brief it and that it wants a response. Counsel indicated that no discovery is needed. The Petition for Instructions on Demand for Reimbursement to Department of Health Care Services is continued to October 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel are to set their own briefing schedule.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Reporter: Jennifer Roux, CSR #11033, 877-451-1580, jroux@aikenwelch.com
